steps.title: Identify the vertex
steps.content:
The equation $$y = -16(x - 6.8)^2 + 740$$ is in vertex form $$y = a(x - h)^2 + k$$ with $$a = -16$$, $$h = 6.8$$, and $$k = 740$$.
Thus the vertex is $$(h, k) = (6.8, 740)$$.
Since $$a \lt 0$$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex gives the maximum height.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex and match to choice
steps.content:
The vertex $$(6.8, 740)$$ means the firework reaches its maximum estimated height of 740 feet at 6.8 seconds after launch.
This matches the description in choice B.
That confirms B is correct.
